entry.title: Re: How to automate or simplify the mass creation of loca...

entry.title: Re: Bookmarks in Fiori Report
entry.content:
Hi @alina_chira2,

yes, you can transport bookmarks to other systems by executing ABAP-Report FPM_BICS_TRANSPORT_BOOKMARKS.

You can assign the bookmarks on a transport with this report and in the target system you can import the bookmarks (after importing the transport) with the ABAP-Report FPM_BICS_IMPORT_BOOKMARKS.

 

Best Regards,

Sabrina

entry.title: Re: Fixed asset migration for French company
entry.content:
Hello @ZuzanaGersiova ,

In parallel ledger setup:

Area 01 (0L) manages APC
Area 93 (2L) derives values
When migration tries to push APC + depreciation separately into area 93, system recalculates derived values
Rounding (848 vs 847.99) + derived logic may create temporary negative NBV → simulation fails

Since asset is economically fully depreciated, safest approach:

-Load APC 848
-Load cumulative depreciation 848
-Let system calculate NBV = 0

Soilution:

-Verify if area 93 is “derived” and does NOT manage APC.
-Remove APC values for area 93 in migration template.
-Ensure asset is fully depreciated as of migration key date (if correct).
-Re-run simulation.

In S/4HANA Cloud:

Only leading depreciation area should carry manual APC during migration.
Parallel areas derive automatically based on configuration.
Manual values in derived areas trigger APC inconsistency errors.

 

Situation / QuestionExplanation, Root Cause & SAP Notes
0L simulation successful, 2L (area 93) failsDepreciation area 93 (France-specific) is typically configured as derived area and may not manage APC (Acquisition and Production Cost) separately. If it is set as “does not post APC”, system rejects APC values during migration.
Error: “Depreciation area 93 does not manage APC values”In France localization, area 93 often mirrors values from another area (e.g., 01 or 02) and is not configured to accept independent acquisition postings. Migration file is attempting to post APC values directly into area 93. 📌 SAP Note 2453614 – Depreciation Area Behavior in New Asset Accounting 📌 SAP Note 2248229 – New Asset Accounting Parallel Valuation Logic
Error: “Acquisition value will become negative in depreciation area 93”Because the area does not manage APC, system tries to derive values. When cumulative depreciation nearly equals APC (848 – 847.99), rounding differences in derived area logic may create inconsistency, causing simulated negative net book value.
Why only 2L affectedArea 93 is linked to ledger 2L (local ledger). In Public Cloud, depreciation areas are strictly tied to ledger logic. If area is configured as “no APC management”, you must not load APC in that area.
Migration key date 31.12.2025Since useful life is 4 years starting 01.02.2020, asset should be fully depreciated by 31.01.2024. At migration date 31.12.2025, NBV should normally be zero. Loading 1 cent residual may conflict with derived area rules.
Likely Root CauseMigration template includes values for all depreciation areas. For derived areas (like 93), you must not populate APC separately; values must derive automatically from leading area.
What to Change in Migration File✔ Enter APC and depreciation only for leading depreciation area (e.g., area 01 / 0L). ✔ Leave area 93 values blank if it is derived. ✔ Ensure cumulative depreciation equals APC (if fully depreciated).
Check ConfigurationIn SSCUI → Define Depreciation Areas: verify whether area 93 is marked as “Post APC” or “Adopt values from area xx”. If it adopts values, no independent values allowed.
France Specific SetupIn FR localization, area 93 often used for special reporting (e.g., CRC rules) and may be technical/derived. Confirm ledger group assignment.
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help → Data Migration for Asset Accounting SAP Help → Parallel Valuation in New Asset Accounting
Relevant SAP Notes (Verify Version Applicability)📌 SAP Note 2591969 – Ledger-Specific Asset Values in S/4HANA 📌 SAP Note 2248229 – Parallel Accounting in New Asset Accounting 📌 SAP Note 2453614 – Depreciation Area Does Not Manage APC 📌 SAP Note 3068705 – Asset Migration Errors in S/4HANA Cloud

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Integrated Toolchain: LeanIX + SAP Cloud ALM: Auto-Discovering Your SAP Landscape
entry.content:
In my previous blog Here "Why the SAP Integrated Toolchain Is the Future of Digital Transformation"  I introduced the Integration Gap the idea that most SAP transformation programs have the right tools: LeanIX, Signavio, WalkMe, Cloud ALM, Tricentis, Syniti  but haven't connected them. And with it, the Digital Thread that flows through the toolchain when they are. We start activating that thread here, with LeanIX connected to SAP Cloud ALM an integration that gives you an accurate, automatically maintained view of your SAP landscape without any manual effort.

 

The Problem: You Can't Transform What You Can't See

Organisations need full transparency into their SAP landscape to effectively prepare and execute transformation initiatives. Yet in practice, that transparency is almost never there at the start of a program.

The typical approach such as stakeholder surveys, spreadsheets, email chains, manual fact sheet updates is slow and structurally unreliable. SAP landscapes change constantly: cloud services are provisioned, on-premise systems are upgraded, integrations are added or retired. By the time a manually assembled inventory is considered complete, it is already out of date. Blind spots are baked in from day one.

"The most expensive thing an Enterprise Architect can do is make decisions

based on a landscape that no longer exists."

The LeanIX–Cloud ALM integration was built to solve this problem at the source, replacing the manual update cycle with continuous, automated discovery.

What the Integration Does

The SAP Cloud ALM integration enables you to import "projects" from SAP Cloud ALM () into SAP LeanIX as fact
sheets and export "initiatives" from SAP LeanIX to () SAP Cloud ALM. It streamlines the workflow for those who
use SAP Cloud ALM by allowing them to continue with enterprise architecture initiatives in SAP LeanIX.

 

The integration delivers two distinct capabilities through a single configuration both powered by the same service key created in the SAP BTP cockpit.

[ Diagram: SAP Landscape Discovery — LeanIX + Cloud ALM architecture ]

Capability 1 — SAP Landscape Discovery in LeanIX 

The SAP Landscape Discovery feature in SAP LeanIX is an automated, out-of-the-box integration designed to identify and map an organization's SAP systems and services directly into their LeanIX inventory. By leveraging SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management), this feature reduces the manual effort of documenting complex SAP environments, providing a comprehensive, real-time "as-is" view of the landscape. 

Fot Deep dive for this case Check: Introducing SAP Landscape Discovery in SAP LeanIX by @fabianlehmann  

 

Key aspects of the feature include:
 
Automated Discovery & Mapping: The tool automatically identifies SaaS, On-Premise, and ERP systems, reducing manual data collection and minimizing human error.

Discovery Inbox: Discovered items appear in a dedicated "Discovery Inbox," where they can be reviewed and either linked to existing Application Fact Sheets or used to create new ones.

Support for SAP BTP: The feature includes support for discovering SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) services, helping map cloud-based extensions.

Accelerated Transformation: It provides immediate visibility for ERP transformation projects (e.g., migrating to SAP S/4HANA) by highlighting dependencies and identifying "clean core" opportunities.

Continuous Updates: The integration ensures that the LeanIX workspace remains up-to-date as the SAP landscape changes.

Key Benefits:

Eliminates Blind Spots: Provides a 360-degree view of all SAP systems and services.
Saves Time: Automates the creation and maintenance of Fact Sheets.

Data Accuracy: Improves the accuracy of the IT landscape data.

Integration with Strategy: Helps align technical SAP data with business capabilities, allowing for better strategic decision-making in transformations

Capability 2 — Project Synchronisation

The second capability connects LeanIX's "initiative" planning layer to Cloud ALM's project execution layer. "Projects" from Cloud ALM can be imported into LeanIX as Initiative Fact Sheets either creating new fact sheets or linking to existing ones. The direction of synchronisation is Cloud ALM → LeanIX: any changes made to Cloud ALM projects are automatically reflected in the linked fact sheets.

LeanIX initiatives can also be exported to Cloud ALM to create new projects. This export is one-way subsequent changes to the initiative fact sheet are not reflected back in Cloud ALM.

[ Diagram: Project Synchronisation — data flow and fact sheet fields ]

The following data is written onto the Initiative Fact Sheet from Cloud ALM:

  • On Track / Needs Attention / Critical: Project Status
  • Project completion in %
  • Active phase set to earliest Cloud ALM timebox date; End-of-life phase set to latest timebox date: Lifecycle dates
  • Milestones

Why It Matters

This integration produces two shifts in how the EA function operates and they compound each other.

The first is where EA time goes In a manually maintained landscape, a significant portion of EA effort is absorbed by data upkeep — chasing system owners, reconciling conflicting information, updating fact sheets. With Landscape Discovery active, that effort drops to significantly. EA time shifts from maintaining the picture to analysing it.

The second is decision quality. Architectural decisions ,application rationalization, migration sequencing and technical debt prioritization are only as good as the data behind them. When that data is continuously maintained and automatically accurate, the quality of every downstream decision improves. Blind spots are eliminated. Hidden dependencies surface. Risks that previously went undetected become visible before they become problems.

With Project Synchronisation active(), the EA team also gains live delivery visibility seeing real-time Cloud ALM project progress directly within the architectural context of LeanIX, without switching tools or chasing status updates.

 

Real-World Case Study: Significant Time Reduction

A large organisation preparing for S/4HANA needed a complete, accurate application inventory as the foundation for their business case and migration sequencing plan. Using traditional methods, this exercise had previously taken several weeks

With Landscape Discovery active, the EA team had a populated, accurate inventory within fraction of the time. 43 SAP services and systems were discovered and mapped automatically including three cloud services that had been provisioned without the EA team's knowledge.

As the program progressed, Cloud ALM project milestones synced automatically into LeanIX initiative fact sheets. The EA team had live delivery visibility across the program without a single manual status update.

Benefits at a Glance

[ Diagram: Benefits of SAP Cloud ALM Integration for Managing Projects ]

  • Minimal effort import - initiatives and projects are brought in directly, with no manual duplication across platforms
  • Consistent project data - all project-related activities and updates are reflected automatically and consistently in LeanIX
  • Real-time sync - linked fact sheets update continuously as Cloud ALM projects evolve
  • Reduced overhead - less administrative work, fewer data discrepancies, improved efficiency across both tools
  • Full execution visibility - detailed project progress from Cloud ALM is visible inside LeanIX, eliminating tool-switching and status-chasing.

Ready to Activate It?

The setup involves two steps: enabling API access to Cloud ALM and connecting LeanIX to the Cloud ALM API, then reviewing and importing projects. My colleague @annika1998  have published the complete step-by-step technical guide on the SAP Community covering prerequisites, BTP service key creation, and the full LeanIX configuration. I have linked it below: 

 Connecting SAP Cloud ALM and SAP LeanIX - A step by step guide 

 

Setup & Learning Resources

•  Step-by-Step Technical Guide : Connecting SAP Cloud ALM and SAP LeanIX - A step by step guide by @annika1998 

•  SAP Help : Configuring SAP Cloud ALM Integration for Managing Projects: help.sap.com/docs/leanix/ea/configuring-sap-cloud-alm-integration-for-managing-projects

•  SAP Help : Synchronizing Projects Between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP LeanIX: help.sap.com/docs/leanix/ea/sap-discovery-configuring-cloud-alm

•  SAP Help : Landscape Discovery Inbox: help.sap.com/docs/leanix/ea/sap-landscape-discovery-inbox

•  LeanIX Academy- SAP Landscape Discovery course: academy.leanix.net

•  SAP Learning - Working with the Integrated Toolchain: learning.sap.com

 

Finally, have you connected LeanIX to SAP Cloud ALM in your organisation? Share your success story in the comments , I'd love to hear from you and help get your success story Published.

Next: Blog 2 : LeanIX + SAP Signavio

Knowing what systems you have is the foundation. The next step is understanding what those systems actually do ,which business processes they support, where they create bottlenecks, and what happens to those processes when an application changes. That is where the LeanIX + Signavio integration comes in. Blog 2 publishes next.

Ramy Salem

ANZ Toolchain Ambassador at SAP

PhD Candidate – AI Agents for Enterprise Architecture & AI Adoption

Certified in SAP | WalkMe | LeanIX | Signavio |TOGAF

Read the Toolchain Blogs that inspired this series:

  • Why the SAP Integrated Toolchain Is the Future of Digital Transformation
  • How Enterprise Architects Can Unlock Strategic Value with WalkMe in SAP Landscapes
  • How Enterprise Architects Can Unlock Strategic Value with SAP Signavio in SAP Landscapes
  • Bridging Strategy to Execution: How LeanIX, Signavio, and WalkMe Create the Ultimate EA Toolchain

entry.title: Full rundown on next week's SAP Enterprise Architect Learning Forum
entry.content:
Here’s The Rundown On The Next Generation Enterprise Architect Learning Forum

AI isn’t just accelerating change, it’s compounding it. Every industry, every operating model, every role is being reshaped. And next week, at the Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Learning Forum, nearly 500 in-person leaders — plus a global livestream audience of 2,000+ — will gather in Newtown Square, PA to tackle one question:

How do we architect for exponential change?

This isn’t just another conference. It’s a working forum for people who build the future.

What Makes This Forum Different?

Most events talk about AI. This one shows you how to design with it. Here’s what attendees can expect:

  • Eight pre-conference deep dives
  • Five breakout tracks
  • 24 Exploration Hub stations
  • Curated roundtables
  • Peer-driven collaboration sessions
  • Concierge expert connections
  • Expanded networking receptions
  • Global livestream access
  • Professionally recorded sessions (shared post-event)

This isn’t passive listening. It’s immersive architecture.

Day Zero: Depth Before Noise

The Forum has expanded pre-conference programming because surface-level AI conversations aren’t enough anymore. Here’s what you can expect on Day Zero, February 23rd:

Morning Deep Dives

  • The Human Agentic AI Edge
  • Data Strategy with Sebastian Wernicke
  • LeanIX 101 + Customer Stories

Afternoon Intensives

  • Your SAP Business AI Edge
  • Enterprise Data Masterclass
  • Application Rationalization
  • SAP Security: Architecture, Governance & Trust
  • The Multidimensional Architect

Track 1 = Full-day AI
Track 2 = Full-day Data

In addition, attendees will notice the topic of security is a recurring theme and runs through everything, not as an afterthought, but as architecture.

Day Zero ends with a Welcome Reception where conversations and networking continue, the ideal pre-game warm up before the main event.

Day One: Strategy at the Table

The Auditorium Track is livestreamed globally and professionally recorded. But being in the room? That’s different.

Highlights include:

  • Ian Beacraft — Architects of Change: Redesigning Work for the AI Era (a preview of his SXSW keynote)
  • Vlad (BCG) & Helmut — The Empty Chair That’s Costing Millions — why architects must sit at the strategy table
  • Collaboration with ASUG, the Chief Architect Network, and the Twin Cities Business Architecture Forum

That evening, the Café transforms into a networking hub sponsored by Onapsis.

SAP Press authors will be signing books including Enterprise Architecture with SAP, BTP, LeanIX, and Business Suite. Sebastian Wernicke will sign copies of The Data Manifesto.

This is where hallway conversations become long-term collaborations.

Day Two: Staying Human in the AI Era

Featured speaker:

Rishad Tobaccowala — Staying Human in the Age of AI

Because amid all this acceleration, the real differentiator is still judgment, creativity, and influence.

Returning favorite:

Stuart Browne — Welcome to the Matrix: Bending Time for SAP Architects

And for the first time ever, a live book launch:

Andreas Welsch — The Human Agentic AI Edge

A forum isn’t just about learning — it’s about shaping the narrative of the profession.

Architecting with Authority

Tuesday at 3:30 PM.

Supported by SAP BWN and sponsored by IBM.

A focused experience inside the Forum on influence, confidence, framing, and impact.

Because technical depth without authority limits your reach.

Day Three: Applied, Practical, Transformational

Highlights include:

  • Architecture lessons from rebuilding Notre Dame Cathedral
  • AI-Augmented Architect (peer show-and-tell)
  • Toolchain deep dive workshops
  • Ferose VR — From IQ to EQ to LQ (Learning Quotient)

And throughout all three days, James Marland will create visual summaries you can take back to your teams — making insights portable and actionable.

The Exploration Hub: Where Theory Meets Practice

24 expert stations featuring:

  • SAP experts
  • Startups like Luzid
  • Partners and sponsors
  • Community organizations
  • Peer networking circles

This is where you pressure-test ideas. Where you meet the people solving the problems you’re wrestling with right now.

Beyond the Event

All sessions will be:

  • Posted to YouTube
  • Summarized via blog
  • Included in a 30 Days of Learning series

And the momentum continues globally:

  • London – April 15
  • Toronto – April 22
  • Additional EA Days of Learning throughout the year

This isn’t a moment. It’s a movement.

Why You Should Be There

Because AI isn’t coming — it’s here.
Because architects who don’t evolve become implementers.
Because strategy tables don’t add empty chairs on their own.

This Forum is where:

  • Architects become influencers
  • Data becomes strategy
  • AI becomes design
  • Community becomes leverage

If you care about your role in the next decade of enterprise transformation, this is the event for you.

And if you’re already registered?

Come ready. Come curious. Come prepared to lead.

The future is exponential. Let’s architect it together!

entry.title: Embedded Launch Activities: Expanding the Customer Onboarding Journey
entry.content:
Embedded Launch Activities: Expanding the Customer Onboarding Journey

From March 2 onwards, SAP expands Embedded Launch Activities with an enhanced onboarding experience designed to accelerate time to value from contract start and enable implementation teams to engage hands-on with the system at the earliest possible stage.

This expanded framework supports all customers subscribing to any SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition bundle and provides a structured onboarding journey through 1:1 sessions, quarterly 1:m live sessions, and self-service resources. As SAP solutions continue to evolve, so does the Embedded Launch Activities framework — continuously expanding in scope, depth, and relevance.

The Customer Onboarding Journey

1:1 Onboarding Sessions

We engage directly with every customer, always involving implementation partners who bring deep expertise in SAP solutions, industry context, and customer business processes.

These sessions establish a shared understanding across all stakeholders, covering:

  • Project scope and key milestones

  • System architecture and provisioning foundations

  • Commercial entitlements included in the subscription

  • An introduction to artificial intelligence capabilities embedded in the product

  • An overview of SAP Support and engagement models

This early alignment lays the groundwork for a confident and well-structured implementation.

1:1 Entitlement Deep-Dive Sessions

As part of the current commercial bundles, customers have access to additional solutions across treasury, expense reporting, sustainability, and extensibility through SAP Build.

To help implementation teams understand both the value and the implementation path of these solutions, dedicated specialist 1:1 sessions are available, including:

  • SAP Build

  • SAP Taulia

  • SAP Concur

  • SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

  • Sustainability solutions within Supply Chain Premium

These sessions ensure that entitlements translate into practical adoption and measurable business outcomes.

Quality Gates with SAP Cloud ALM

A core element of the expanded framework is guided enablement in setting up quality gates within SAP Cloud ALM.

Through shared-screen sessions, customers are guided to:

  • Define critical checkpoints across the project lifecycle

  • Assess readiness and quality of deliverables

  • Validate compliance with requirements

  • Confirm that key tasks and documentation meet expected standards

Quality gates provide structured decision points that support informed go/no-go decisions and reduce delivery risk.

Alignment Through the Onboarding Plan

The onboarding journey concludes with a dedicated 1:1 session focused on alignment around the Onboarding Plan, ensuring clarity on next steps and ongoing support.

Customers are also encouraged to participate in 1:m live sessions (with recordings available), covering topics such as:

  • Project methodology and implementation best practices

  • End-to-end system architecture

  • SAP product roadmaps

  • Influence SAP and SAP Communities

  • Upgrade cycles and continuous innovation

  • Tools that support sustained value from ERP, including ongoing AI innovations

Guided Enablement and Continuous Learning

As part of the Embedded Launch Activities framework, customers also gain access to Guided Enablement, a structured learning program for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition onboarding.

This program helps implementation teams track progress and build capability throughout the project lifecycle. The learning content is continuously updated to reflect product evolution and emerging best practices.

The Digital Baseline

The Onboarding Resource Center serves as the digital baseline for the onboarding journey. Customers are introduced through a welcome email and subsequently contacted by an Onboarding Advisor to initiate the journey and guide them through each phase.

Embedded Launch Activities provide a consistent, scalable, and evolving onboarding experience — designed to support customers from day one and grow alongside their SAP landscape.

entry.title: Collaboration Step | Role-Based Permissions based on Route Steps for Executive Review - only 1 role
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently configuring a Compensation template in SAP SuccessFactors that includes a Collaboration Step in the route map.

When I enable the feature “Role-Based Permissions Based on Route Steps for Executive Review”, I expect to see multiple roles for this Collaboration Step (since Collaboration Steps can contain more than one role).

However, in the Executive Review permission matrix, I only see one single role for the entire Collaboration Step, even though 2 roles are assigned to that step in the route map.

Could you help me please answering following questions?

- Is this expected system behavior?
- Why does the Executive Review permission configuration only display one role for a Collaboration Step?
- Is there a workaround if I need more granular permissions for different participants of the Collaboration Step?

Thanks in advance for your help.

Best Regards,
Johannes

entry.title: Physical Stock Negative
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am facing a stock inconsistency in Embedded EWM and would like to understand if anyone has experienced something similar.

Scenario:

  • QM is active
    • Stock type Q is NOT used (stock is always F – Unrestricted Use)
    • GR from external vendor creates an inspection lot
    • Initial storage type is HU-managed
    • Stock is moved via WT to a NON HU-managed storage type
    • During WT confirmation, the HU is dissolved

Issue:

After WT confirmation:

  • Total stock for the material/batch is correct (both EWM and ERP)
    • Available quantity is correct
    • No queue errors
    • However, a negative quant (e.g. -1600 PCS) is created

The negative quantity is visible in:
• /SCWM/QUAN
• /LIME/NQUAN

Another positive quant compensates the total stock, so overall quantity remains correct.

Important detail:

The negative quant is the only one that still carries the reference to the EWM inspection document.

When entering the inspection document in EWM, the system shows a negative open quantity to be decisioned, which obviously cannot be processed.

It seems that during HU dissolution and quant recreation, the inspection reference was not properly reassigned, resulting in a negative quant still linked to the inspection document.

Questions:

  1. Has anyone experienced negative quants at LIME level after HU-managed → non-HU-managed movement?
  2. Is this a known behavior related to quant reassignment during HU dissolution?
  3. Are there specific configuration settings (WPT or storage type) that could lead to this situation?

Any feedback would be greatly appreciated before opening an OSS incident.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone: Tile Opens(content federation) but App Fails with ‘IP Address Cannot Be Fou
Re: IRRF PF - Lei 15.270/2025
entry.content:
Hello @2005415254 

For multiple invoices in the same month:

-First invoice → IRRF calculated on its base.
-Second invoice → system recalculates IRRF based on accumulated monthly base.
-Previously withheld amount is considered.
-Only the difference is posted on the second invoice.

This ensures compliance with progressive IRRF taxation rules.

Test Scenario

1. Create vendor as “Individual Contributor” with IRRF tax type active.
2. Post Invoice 1 (same month).
3. Post Invoice 2 (same month).
4. Validate:
-Withholding tax base (BSEG/QSSHB equivalent in ACDOCA)
-Accumulated base value
-IRRF amount difference calculation
5. Reverse and repost to test edge cases.

Validation

After implementing Lei 15.270/2025 SAP Notes, confirm:
Updated IRRF tax tables are correctly loaded.
Calculation procedure reflects new brackets.
Monthly accumulation logic is active.

 

Scenario / QuestionExplanation, Test Approach & SAP Notes
Individual service provider (Pessoa Física) with IRRFIn Brazil localization, IRRF is calculated via Extended Withholding Tax functionality. Accumulation depends on configuration of withholding tax type and accumulation period (monthly).
More than one NF in same monthIf withholding tax type is configured with accumulation = monthly, the system must cumulate the taxable base of all invoices within the same calendar month before recalculating IRRF.
Concern: Is IRRF recalculated progressively?Yes. When properly configured, SAP recalculates IRRF considering prior base amount already posted in same month and adjusts the withholding amount in the second invoice.
Example Test ScenarioInvoice 1 (10/03): BRL 5,000 → IRRF calculated normally. Invoice 2 (20/03): BRL 4,000 → System should calculate IRRF on cumulative BRL 9,000 and subtract IRRF already withheld in Invoice 1.
Key Configuration Check✔ Withholding Tax Type: accumulation indicator active ✔ Accumulation period = monthly ✔ “Base amount cumulation” enabled ✔ Valid IRRF tax tables per Lei 15.270/2025 maintained
Where to VerifySPRO → Financial Accounting → Withholding Tax → Extended Withholding Tax → Calculation → Define Withholding Tax Types
If accumulation not happeningUsually caused by: • Incorrect accumulation period • Vendor master not marked for withholding tax • Different company code or different withholding tax type used • Missing configuration from SAP Notes implementing Lei 15.270/2025 updates
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help → Withholding Tax (Brazil) – Extended Withholding Tax SAP Help → Accumulation of Withholding Tax Base Amounts
Relevant SAP Notes (Verify Applicability)📌 SAP Note 3383457 – IRRF Adjustments According to Lei 15.270/2025 📌 SAP Note 3354678 – Brazil IRRF Calculation Update 📌 SAP Note 1706300 – Withholding Tax Accumulation Logic 📌 SAP Note 2220152 – Extended Withholding Tax in S/4HANA


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Can Custom Alternative Material Logic Be Embedded in...
entry.content:
Hello @zhangtaiping 

Thank you for your question.

Your customer is asking for planning layer substitution. In Public Cloud, you generally cannot replace the MRP engine logic with arbitrary custom code. The feasible approaches are therefore master data driven and not custom algorithm inside MRP.

Question: Within the constraints of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, are there any innovative or feasible approaches to support this requirement?

Answer: Yes, but the innovation is in modelling, not in custom MRP logic. This would require you to:

  • Use BOM alternatives and let MRP consider them with the standard behaviour.
  • Use validity dates, priorities, and policies to drive which alternative is planned.

See Blog: Concept of Alternative Item group functionality in BOM and

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/82cc8757c483ad6be10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US for more details.

Question: Can the BAdI PPH_SUPPLY_DEMAND_LIST be leveraged to implement this scenario—i.e., dynamically inject supply/demand elements based on the customer’s substitution logic during the MRP run?

Answer: You can use it to modify the supply and demand list elements, and those changes can be considered by MRP Live. But using it as a complete replacement for “custom substitution logic inside MRP that derives procurement proposals across materials” is typically not the right tool and it is risky in Public Cloud because it is not clean core.

See Debugging transaction MD04 in SAP S/4HANA

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Connectivity issue OAuth 2.0 between SAP CPI to Sales...
entry.content:
For the Security Token Alias, you must combine the password of the Salesforce user (PW: abc) and the associated security token (Sec-Token: 123) — which is sent automatically whenever the password is changed.
The combined value must then be created as the Security Token Alias (example: abc123).

entry.title: Re: Collaboration Step | Role-Based Permissions based on...
entry.content:
Hi @Joe0603 

The "Role-Based Permissions Based on Route Steps for Executive Review" only works for one workflow step at a time, and a collaboration step is only one step with several accesses inside of it. So yes this is expected behavior.

I usually stay away from using collaboration steps for Compensation processes. If you want several people to access at the same time then you should create a Comp Admin step (all forms are routed to comp admin user) and open executive review access to all these roles while forms are in that step. Leading practice worklow is documented here.

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Tips while configuring SAP Asset accounting and Data upload.
entry.content:
Recently i got a chance to work in 1 project where my role was of Asset Data migration lead. I had to monitor the uploads of the Asset Data.  In each project i learn something and i document it for future reference. I convert that document into blog and share with my community so that they also can benefit from it. 

Disclaimer - This blog does not reflect my expertise but my experience. I received lot of support from my Senior and Junior team members and Data migration was successful. 

Alone and we will sink, collectively and we will sail through. 

So, let's begin.

 

  • Negative values allowed for depreciation area.

When you configure asset accounting, Chart of Depreciation area and depreciation area is must. Most important point while configuring Depreciation areas is to enable it for negative values. Once you do this, assets will be allowed for negative posting. 

During our data upload, we uploaded few assets incorrectly. We uploaded without negative values tick mark, and this was creating a problem. System was not allowed to reverse the asset document in AB08. Now all assets’ masters were enabled for negative postings, and it allowed them to reverse, except 1 sub-number asset.

Asset sub-number was deriving values from Main Asset. So, when we check negative value box in Main number, sub number asset error message was reflecting. When we try to check negative value box in Sub Number asset, Main asset error message was reflected.

So always mark negative values for all depreciation areas to avoid such problems.

 

  • Real and derived areas should have same values.

When you configure the depreciation area, make sure all values in Real areas and Derived areas are similar.  For e.g. 01 and 30. 01 is Real and 30 is derived from 01. In 01, if Acquisition values are ALL VALUES ALLOWED, then 30 should have same values. I know this is very simple and basic, but simples’ things tend to be missed more. So, check all this in detail.

 

  • Sub number asset derived field should be editable.

Generally, we derive values in sub number from Main Asset number, and this is valid too. But those fields are non-editable, and this may create last minute problems. As mentioned above, we tried to reverse the Asset and Sub Asset, but it was not allowed to reverse due to negative postings. Also, it was not allowed to save both the assets after checking Negative values Box.

Reason, in Asset main number, depreciation key was 0001 and in sub asset number it was 0001. We wanted to change to 0002 but field was not modified. If field would be editable, this problem would have been mitigated.

Go to AO21. Sbno. Should be ticked.

 

  • All Depreciation area in asset master

Depreciation area configured in OADB should reflect in Asset master in AS03. If it is not reflected, it is a concern. I know this is basic, but this can be missed.  Check for all company codes why this depreciation area is not coming. Because moving transport at the last moment may create lot of problems. If you do not see the depreciation area, go to OAYZ and check if it is deactivated. If yes, activate.

Note : In ECC, there was AFAN tcode to activate all depreciation area but I could not found this in S/4. We need to activate one by one for each Asset Class.

 

  • Real Depreciation Area Tick Mark.

When configuration is done, check Real area tick mark is made only for Real Depreciation Area. For derived depreciation area there should be no tick mark. If you try to change this at later point of time, this will create problem. So do check this beforehand.

 

  • Data templates should have negative values allowed.

When data is uploaded, instruct your Data team to put X in negative values box for all Asset master’s file. With this, your asset master is now enabled for negative postings. This will help you at the time of reversal of asset.

 

  • Bot should be configured for exact date.

This could be a big issue for Data Migration team. For e.g. extraction date is 31.12.2025. so you should get data as on 31.12.2025. Cumulative value should have data, but depreciation posted and Transaction data should be blank.

Now what happens, BOT is not configured/customized to extract data for exact date, and this could be a problem.  For e.g. you are on 18-02-2026. Data to be extracted as on 31.12.2025 but BOT is not customized to extract data as on 31.12.2025. So, they will extract 3 sheets for you.

  1. Cumulative value
  2. Depreciation posted
  3. Transaction data.

Data team will spend time in collating above information and there is a big risk, like data mismatch.

 

  •      Data Re-upload

Always be ready for this. Sometimes 

  1. cost center is incorrect or
  2. sub number field is not editable or
  3. Depreciation key is incorrect or
  4. Special Depreciation is not reflecting in AW01N or Asset History sheet. 
  5. ......Could be many more.

 

  •     Data Verification.

Sometime client complaints about data is incorrect. They are completely mismatching and you start spending time in reconciliation. At the end you find all data upload is correct and there is no error. So where is the error? 

Ask the user which file you have referred? Extraction file or converted file? Sometimes they reconcile data with the Extracted file from legacy system and not the converted file. This seems very simple, but this will take away lot of time.

 

  •    Data conversion (Most Important)

Never take responsibility of file conversion from the client. Tell client to convert the raw data to uploaded data and share with us to upload. If you take this responsibility, this will create many problems like - 

  1. Time management
  2. Erroneous data
  3. Accountable and responsible.
  4. ......could be many more.

 

  •     Access to legacy system

Make sure you have access to legacy system if you are responsible for data conversion. This will help you verify the data upfront with extracted file.  If you do not have access, you will have dependency upon user to extract data from legacy system. This is very tiring and cumbersome.

So to conclude, there are many more things in data migration which need to be taken care. Above points are one which i fell are important, so shared with you all.

Hope you liked this blog.

Regards

CA Zunaid Hingora

entry.title: Re: ML not active in material master at sap s/4hana publi...
entry.content:
Dear @Ramdhani,

Point 1. Can we activate Material Ledger for materials already extended to the new plant?

Not manually in the material master. In Public Cloud the Material Ledger Active indicator in Accounting 1 is system-derived. If it is blank/greyed out, it means the system did not create the required ML/valuation data for that material + plant (valuation area) at the time the plant extension was created and you cannot switch it on afterwards in the product master

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3311219

Point 2. What is the recommended fix without creating a new material?

You have two standard options, depending on whether there are already postings/stock in the new plant:

Option A: Best if possible
Use this when the material in the new plant has no stock and no postings/documents yet.

1. Correct the configuration in CBC SSCUI 103539 Define Attributes of Material Types and ensure Quantity updating and Value updating are ticked for the relevant material type + new plant

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3508124

1. Remove/recreate the plant-specific valuation/accounting segment (only feasible when there are no dependencies)

2. Extend the material to the plant again the system should then create the ML/valuation data correctly

Option B: If there is already stock/postings
If the material has already been used in the new plant (stock or postings exist) then deleting/recreating plant valuation data is typically not feasible. In Public Cloud the practical and standard route is to raise an SAP incident and reference the Public Cloud symptom based on SAP Note 3311219

Point 3. Is a configuration change required or is re-extending sufficient?

You must correct SSCUI 103539 (Quantity/Value updating for material type + plant). Re-extending alone does not reliably fix materials that were already extended incorrectly it mainly prevents the issue for future extensions

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3508124

IMPORTANT: You can find also a SAP Note 3365959 but is for SAP S/4HANA on-prem/private cloud and refers to customer-executable correction reports. This is not the Public Cloud approach.

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003365959

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Grouping Open Items in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Disclaimer: All images are from internal test systems with usefulness only for demonstrating.

Over the years, changes come and a lot of information needs to be passed on. Today I come through this blog to exemplify a useful business process within the financial context in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Let's say that within your system, you have multiple Open Items with identical fields, and that would make your business\process a lot easier knowing that they will only be processed in an automatic payment run.

This is possible, and of course, with some settings at the level of Business Partner and Payment Method, you can indicate to the system how to specifically coordinate these Open Items by making some checks.

Validation goes through confirmation that the following fields are exactly the same:

  • BP (Supplier / Customer);
  • Currency;
  • Receiver;
  • Payment Method;
  • Bank Details.

Once you confirm that the fields are exactly the same, just go through some settings (SSCUI) and applications in Fiori to confirm the settings done as expected:

In the Maintain Business Partner app, you need to select the Role for Supplier and under Company Code section for Payment Transactions, you could find the following view:

This checkbox must be empty, indicating to the system you will not process a single payment for this specific supplier.

Single Payment: If this indicator is set, every customer/vendor open item is paid separately during automatic payment transactions. This means that open items are not grouped together for payment.

It's important also to check the payment method and company code settings to confirm the grouping of open items.

In the SSCUI Configuration Activity (101044) Set Up Payment Methods per Company Code for Payment Transactions:

Single Payment for Marked Item: If this indicator is set, the system processes items as individual payments when the payment method is explicitly entered. If the payment method is determined by the payment program rather than being manually specified, the system may group multiple items together.

Payment per Due Day: When this flag is set, with one payment only open items are paid that are due on the same day.

Extended Individual Payment:  The open items of a payment group are paid individually according to the rules. It it possible to include and offset all available credit memos for the payment group.

For individual payment (for designated items or according to the master record), however, only credit memos with invoice reference are offset, credit memos without invoice reference cannot be offset.

Example:  

Invoice 1 : 250 USD \ Invoice 2 : 1000 USD \ Invoice 3 : 500 USD \ Invoice 4 : 1000 USD \ Invoice 5 : 1000 USD

Credit Memo 1 : 1000 USD \ Credit Memo 2 : 500 USD \ Credit Memo 3 : 750 USD

All items are contained in a payment group. The items are assigned as follows:

1. Invoice 2 with Credit Memo 1: Cleared to zero

2. Invoice 4 with Credit Memo 3: Payment 250 USD

3. Invoice 3 and Credit Memo 2: Cleared to zero

4. Invoice 1: Payment 250 USD

5. Invoice 5: Payment 1000 USD

You could find this information in the Fiori App Help Documentation.

In the SSCUI 101001 Set Up Paying Company Codes for Payment Transactions this is the screen which you need to look:

Separate Payment for Each Ref: Enabling this flag ensures that only items (invoices and credit memos) with an identical payment reference are processed together in a single payment.

Useful References and Additional Information:

  • 3555465 - Incorrect Splitting of Payment Documents - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me
  • 305414 - F110: Unintelligible grouping of documents - SAP for Me
  • 2490594 - Grouping of items during automatic payment run does not happen as expected - SAP for Me
  • 3592202 - Grouping of open items with reference to Japan in Manage Automatic Payments App - SAP for Me
  • 164835 - F110: Clearing credit memos and invoices - SAP for Me

Thanks you for reading. If you need any clarification, please go through the comments section and share your thoughts!

entry.title: Re: SAP Global Trade Services (GTS): How to Interpret Pre...
entry.content:
Hello @Scott_Klingensmith ,

Value ‘D’ in table /SAPSLL/PRPREF is the internal stored value.
Value ‘B’ shown in /SAPSLL/PROD_PREF_02 is the external (screen) representation defined in the domain or conversion logic.
SAP automatically converts between internal and external values when displaying the field.

Go to SE11
Open table /SAPSLL/PRPREF
Check field PREKE → data element → domain
Review fixed value mapping (Internal vs External column)
You will see how ‘D’ maps to ‘B’.

 

Situation / QuestionExplanation, Technical Logic & SAP Notes
Table /SAPSLL/PRPREF – field PREKE = ‘D’The database table stores the internal domain value of the preference statement indicator. These are technical codes used by GTS logic.
In Tcode /SAPSLL/PROD_PREF_02 → Preference tab shows value ‘B’The screen displays the external (screen) value defined in the domain’s value mapping. SAP converts internal value (‘D’) to external display value (‘B’) using domain fixed values or conversion logic.
Why internal ≠ external value?Many GTS fields use domains where the internal database value differs from the screen value. The screen field uses a conversion exit or domain fixed value mapping.
Where conversion happens technicallyThe mapping is controlled via: • Data element domain (check SE11) • Fixed values assigned in domain • Possible conversion exit in the data element • Screen logic in function group for product preference maintenance
How to verify1️⃣ Check data element of field PREKE in SE11 2️⃣ Open the domain → check fixed values 3️⃣ Compare “Internal Value” vs “External Value” columns
Why GTS does thisGTS stores compact internal codes for processing efficiency and legal determination logic. The UI shows business-readable codes aligned with legal wording.
Does this affect legal determination?No. Determination engine uses internal value. Display value is only UI representation.
Is this standard behavior?Yes, this is standard SAP design for domains with different internal/external representations.
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help → Preference Processing in SAP GTS SAP Help → Product Master for Preference Determination
Relevant SAP Notes (Verify Applicability)📌 SAP Note 1606807 – Preference Determination Field Logic 📌 SAP Note 1458083 – GTS Preference Indicator Mapping 📌 SAP Note 1812484 – Domain Value Display Differences in GTS


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: KSEF QR Code Generation
entry.content:
Hello @RKDY ,

The Invoice Hash (SHA-256) is:

SHA-256 digest of the exact KSeF XML file submitted
Encoded in Base64URL format

It must be generated programmatically from the final XML stored in the eDocument framework, not manually derived from invoice fields.

Technical Flow

Invoice posted in FI/SD
eDocument (DRC) generates final KSeF XML
XML stored in eDocument tables
Retrieve exact XML payload
Compute SHA-256 hash
Convert to Base64URL
Build QR string structure
Render QR in invoice output form

Mistakes to Avoid

-Hashing formatted PDF instead of XML
-Hashing XML before final namespace adjustments
-Ignoring UTF-8 encoding consistency
-Using standard Base64 instead of Base64URL

 

Question / RequirementExplanation, Technical Guidance & SAP Notes
What is the “Invoice hash (SHA-256)” used in KSeF QR code?It is the SHA-256 hash of the exact XML invoice content submitted to KSeF, encoded in Base64URL format. It ensures integrity of the structured e-invoice.
Where does the hash come from in SAP?SAP does not store a ready “QR hash” field in accounting tables. The hash must be calculated from the final XML payload sent to KSeF (after mapping and formatting).
Which XML should be hashed?The final FA(2)/FA(3) XML file exactly as transmitted to KSeF (without any additional formatting changes). Any difference (spaces, line breaks, encoding) will produce a different hash.
How to retrieve XML in SAP?If using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC), the XML is stored in eDocument framework tables and accessible via the eDocument cockpit (e.g., EDOC_COCKPIT or related CDS views). Extract the exact XML sent. 📌 SAP Note 3279206 – Poland KSeF Implementation Overview 📌 SAP Note 3346323 – eDocument Poland Troubleshooting
How to calculate SHA-256 in SAP (ABAP)?Use class CL_ABAP_MESSAGE_DIGEST (available in S/4HANA) to compute SHA-256 on the XML string. Then encode to Base64 and convert to Base64URL (replace + with -, / with _, remove padding =).
Example ABAP logic (simplified)1️⃣ Read XML as STRING in UTF-8 2️⃣ Convert to XSTRING 3️⃣ cl_abap_message_digest=>calculate_hash_for_raw( algorithm = 'SHA-256' ) 4️⃣ Convert result to Base64 5️⃣ Apply Base64URL formatting
Why Base64URL and not standard Base64?Polish KSeF QR specification requires URL-safe Base64 (RFC 4648). This avoids “+” and “/” characters in QR payload.
Important – Do NOT hash accounting dataHash must be generated from the exact XML submitted to KSeF, not from invoice number or PDF output. Hash is document-content based, not header-based.
If using external middlewareIf XML is transformed externally (e.g., CPI, PI/PO), hash must be calculated on the final transmitted version, not pre-mapped SAP version.
Where to embed QR codeQR is typically generated during output form processing (Adobe Form / Smartform / Output Management). Hash must be available in form context before rendering QR.
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help → Document and Reporting Compliance for Poland (KSeF) SAP Help → eDocument Framework
Relevant SAP Notes (Verify Applicability)📌 SAP Note 3279206 – Electronic Document Processing for Poland – KSeF 📌 SAP Note 3346323 – eDocument Poland Known Issues 📌 SAP Note 2134248 – eDocument Framework Overview 📌 SAP Note 3081998 – Poland KSeF Integration Updates


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Error ' Essential transfer parameters are missing in...
entry.content:
Dear @kkumara,

It usually means the system still can’t determine the required transfer/shipping parameters at runtime, i.e. the customizing is not being hit, is incomplete or other prerequisites are missing

I have prepared a checklist for verification, if this does not help, you can report an SAP Incident on SAP for Me portal:

1. Confirm the record was maintained for the exact key used by MF60:
MF60 especially with SLoc Level uses the issuing (EWM) storage location as the source key. If the record was maintained for another SLoc/plant or only a generic entry the system won’t find it and VL561 remains

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3408921

2. Validate shipping point determination prerequisites (rule + assignment):
In Public Cloud, shipping point determination depends on the configured rule + assignments:

  • In CBC search for activity 102385 (Define Rule), 102126 (Assign Shipping Points) and if storage-location-specific is used (102387)

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3546236

3. Check the fallback logic plant/SLoc vs plant default:
SAP confirms the system first tries Plant+SLoc and if not found it defaults from Plant. So if your Plant default is blank/invalid or the Plant+SLoc entry isn’t actually found, VL561 still happens

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3067316

4. In CBC 180019 Define Shipping Data for Stock Transfers Between Storage Locations, ensure all required fields in that activity are maintained (not only Shipping Point):
Open the relevant entry and confirm that all fields marked as mandatory are filled for your Plant + issuing (EWM) storage location and receiving storage location if required. If any mandatory field is blank, VL561 can still occur

5. Master data prerequisites:
Staging delivery creation can also fail if required default partner / organizational data is missing, e.g. Goods Recipient / Ship-to determination or organizational assignments needed for delivery creation such as shipping point relevance. Verify the relevant master data/defaults for the delivery-based stock transfer scenario in your tenant

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3605630

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Elimination of SAP PI PO systems
entry.content:
Hello. I'm creating this post so we can all share our opinions constructively.

I recently learned that SAP plans to eliminate SAP PI/PO systems to focus solely on Integration Suite.

As someone who works extensively with PO, I feel saddened and angry with SAP. After all these years of
working and continuously acquiring knowledge in PO, SAP seems to have decided to kick it to the curb.
All that knowledge feels like throwing it away. Similarly, SAP is forcing clients who have already invested
money in implementing PO to migrate to Integration Suite. I believe many clients don't want the cloud and prefer
to keep their servers on-premises for security or other reasons. However, SAP doesn't care and is forcing all
clients to move their infrastructure to the cloud... and of course, spend money again.
Personally, I find this despicable.

And although I work with PO, I also work with Integration Suite. I find IS awful, much more difficult,
since you have to develop the business logic by creating huge and complicated iFlows, while in PO,
integrations are generally point-to-point, making them easier to implement and understand.
And then there are options like the Monitor, which is rubbish compared to the PO monitor, where it shows
you all the messages, their log traces, payloads... without you having to do anything, by default.
In IS, if you want to see anything in the monitor, you have to develop it yourself to save headers,
log files, payloads, etc., wasting your time.

Anyway, I find it incredible that SAP has played this game with its customers, forcing them to switch.
And of course, it's forcing us, the employees, to learn another way of doing integrations, throwing away
our knowledge. (I just hope SAP doesn't do very well.)

What do you think?
What's your opinion?
Are you for or against it?

Let's see if we can start a thread to discuss this and get it to someone at SAP.

Thanks. Regards.

entry.title: Re: Periode verschieben für Buchungskreis in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @Benedikt_Pantoja,

Yes for Public Cloud the correct way to shift the period is open the next period / close the old one is:

FI (G/L, AP, AR) posting periods:

Use Manage Posting Periods (App ID F2293) to open/close FI posting periods. SAP explicitly states posting period status is maintained with this app

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cdccca8e03d74101a0135863bc522b49/3a06c5536a51204be10000000a174cb4.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2293')/S35

If you can’t open/close periods, it is often role/authorization related. SAP provides a dedicated SAP Note for Manage Posting Periods authorization

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003308006

Important Public Cloud nuance:

  • In Public Cloud, FI period control is driven by the Posting Period Variant (PPV) assigned to the company code
  • In the standard delivery SAP generally has one PPV per company code, so changing the PPV effectively changes that company code
  • Since CE2502, SAP also supports assigning multiple company codes to one PPV so  t’s meaning your change affects all company codes sharing that PPV

SAP Notes below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3598811

If you can’t open/close periods, it’s usually authorization/role related there is a dedicated SAP note about F2293 authorizations

SAP Notes below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002598676

CO (Controlling) posting periods:

For CO period locks (by business transaction types, etc.) the Public Cloud standard is Manage Posting Periods Cost Accounting (App ID F4684), not F2293

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/044eb1694b564390a6479c62497b6bc1.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4684')/S35

MM / Inventory (goods movements) posting periods:

Use Close Periods (MM period) SAP explains this app controls the open MM posting period and typically keeps current/previous open

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/how-works-the-close-periods-app-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13549042

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-sourcing-and-procurement/opening-new-posting-period_e58522bf-3ba2-4b88-a896-efbe59973965

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: MRP Planned Orders Dates & Detailed Material coverage
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Could you please advise where we can configure the system so that, when executing MRP, planned orders can be created with past requirement dates or adjusted opening dates?

Currently, when planned orders are generated with the current date, customers find it difficult to prioritize which planned order should be processed first. If the system could propose dates in the past (in line with requirement dates), it would help clarify priorities.

Additionally, we would like to better understand how the opening date is determined — particularly the time interval between converting a planned order and the actual production start date.

Furthermore, could you recommend a report or CDS view or app similar to MD04 that provides detailed time-phased information for all products, including:

  • Total requirements
  • Available stock
  • Purchase/production orders
  • Quantity shortages by period

The Monitor Material Coverage app only displays the first projected shortage and does not provide a complete time-phased overview and you should go product by product to see the details.

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

Best regards,

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Supply Chain 

 

@Lakshmipathi  @Markus_Oertelt @Caetano

entry.title: SAP Variant Configuration & Pricing Integration with SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management-CALM
entry.content:
Why Integrating SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing with SAP Cloud ALM Matters More Than Ever

SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing integrates with SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (SAP Cloud ALM) and enables using the following features:

  • Integration Monitoring
    You can view messages from the following components centrally:
    • Variant Configuration service
    • Pricing service
    • Data replication
  • Real User Monitoring
    You can see the performance of API calls to the following services:
    • Variant Configuration service
    • Pricing service

Select Scope and Select Service Type - SAP Variant Configuration & Pricing

1. Achieve Centralized Monitoring Across Your Landscape

SAP Cloud ALM provides a central cockpit where teams can monitor communications across VCP components. According to current standard process, the following are fully monitored in one place:

  • Variant Configuration service
  • Pricing service
  • Data replication 
Having this single point of truth eliminates the need to shuffle between logs, systems, or custom monitoring tools. It improves operational efficiency and drastically reduces troubleshooting time.

2. Detect Integration Issues Faster with Integration & Exception Monitoring

Integration processes involving VCP often pass through multiple cloud services, external systems, and business applications. Any delay or failure in message flow can disrupt order processing, pricing calculations, and product configuration consistency.

With Cloud ALM:

  • Messages to and from VCP are captured and classified.
  • Integration exceptions can be filtered by status, configuration IDs, document IDs, and additional parameters.
  • IT teams/VCP Experts in your team can rapidly isolate root causes.

SAP’s VCP monitoring confirms that Integration & Exception Monitoring includes category‑specific insights for VCP, helping identify message failures or anomalies early. 

4. Enable Proactive Operations Through SAP‑Managed Connectivity

Moving data into Cloud ALM doesn’t require custom middleware or manual telemetry setup. VCP uses SAP‑Managed Connectivity—SAP’s built‑in OpenTelemetry‑based infrastructure.

This means:

  • Setup is streamlined; only monitoring activation is required.
  • Data flow to Cloud ALM is secure and compliant.
  • Continuous telemetry is provided with minimal administrative overhead.

SAP Help documentation provides you steps that activating monitoring in Cloud ALM apps is enough to begin data collection. 

5. Strengthen Your Digital Transformation with Lifecycle Transparency

Integration with Cloud ALM is not just about monitoring—it is about operational excellence.

By linking VCP and Cloud ALM, organizations gain:

  • Full visibility into operational health
  • Insights across the entire configuration and pricing chain
  • Support for agile, cloud‑first lifecycle management

As SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve as the central operations and lifecycle tool for SAP cloud solutions, integrating VCP ensures your configuration and pricing processes are aligned with SAP’s roadmap. The integration setup documentation explicitly highlights these monitoring scenarios.

6. A Must‑Have for Production Security Teams: Proactive Detection of Errors, Threats, and API Anomalies

In a production environment, security and reliability become non‑negotiable. Integrating SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing (VCP) with SAP Cloud ALM empowers security and operations teams to continuously monitor for:

  • Failed or suspicious API calls
  • Warnings or error messages spikes
  • Unexpected behavior in configuration or pricing service interactions
  • Patterns that may indicate threats or misuse

SAP Cloud ALM’s Integration Monitoring allows central visibility into messages exchanged across the Variant Configuration service, Pricing service, and data replication components. This helps teams quickly identify anomalies, failed message exchanges, or abnormal traffic patterns. 

Additionally, Real User Monitoring surfaces performance characteristics of API calls to both services, including latency issues or unusual load, which can serve as early warning signs of security or stability problems. 

Together, these capabilities give your security team the observability needed to:

  • Detect potential threats originating from abnormal API traffic
  • React quickly to production errors before they escalate
  • Maintain compliance and audit readiness
  • Strengthen the system’s overall security posture

In highly dynamic configuration and pricing scenarios—where APIs are called hundreds or thousands of times per minute—this level of monitoring is not just beneficial; it is essential for protecting your production environment.

Conclusion: A Small Integration with a Big Impact

Connecting SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing with SAP Cloud ALM is more than a technical convenience—it is a strategic enabler. Organizations benefit from:

  • Faster issue detection and resolution
  • Better performance insights
  • Enhanced transparency
  • Simplified lifecycle management
  • Improved customer experience

Whether you're managing complex configurable products or ensuring accurate pricing across global channels, this integration provides the operational control needed to keep everything running smoothly.

 

Watch out for the next blog for SAP Variant Configuration & Pricing integrated with SAP Cloud ALM benefits.

entry.title: Exploring and Configuring Joule Agents in SAP CX: A Three‑Part Video Series
entry.content:
The rapid evolution of AI‑driven automation is redefining how organizations deliver customer experiences. Within SAP Customer Experience, Joule agents represent one of the most significant shifts—bringing intelligence, autonomy, and business context into everyday workflows across Sales and Service Cloud.
To help teams understand what agents can truly achieve in practice, I created a three‑part video series that walks through both the functional possibilities and the technical foundation behind configuring and extending agents in SAP CX. This series offers a clear and practical view into the end‑to‑end lifecycle of agent usage, from real business scenarios to hands‑on customization.
You can access the full playlist here:
https://learning.sap.com/video-playlists/exploring-and-configuring-joule-agents-in-sap-cx

Why Agents Matter Right Now

Organizations are under increasing pressure to respond to customers faster, personalize interactions, and remove friction from sales and service processes. Yet, many teams still struggle with manual tasks, disconnected systems, and inconsistent execution.
Joule agents address these challenges by operating as intelligent digital teammates. They can understand context, execute tasks, orchestrate data across systems, and guide users through complex steps. The goal is not to replace human expertise but to elevate it—enabling more time for meaningful customer engagement.
This video series was designed to make this vision concrete. Instead of broad theory, the sessions focus on scenarios that matter to frontline teams and show exactly how agents can be shaped to fit unique business needs.

What the Three‑Part Series Covers

1. Real Business Use Cases for Agents in Sales and Service Cloud

The first video explores where agents provide the highest impact across customer‑facing roles. Through practical examples, it demonstrates how agents can support tasks such as lead qualification, opportunity preparation, service case triage, knowledge retrieval, and guided troubleshooting.
This session sets the stage by showing why these capabilities matter and how they align with the daily challenges of sales and service organizations.

2. Customizing a Standard, Pre‑Delivered Agent

The second video takes a step deeper by breaking down the components of a pre‑delivered agent. It shows how to adapt and configure standard behavior to match organizational processes, without the need for heavy development.
From modifying prompts and adjusting workflows to tuning business logic, this video highlights how quickly teams can tailor agents to be more relevant and more effective.

3. Building a Brand‑New Custom Agent

The final video in the series focuses on creating a custom agent from the ground up. This includes defining the agent’s purpose, shaping its actions, setting up integrations, and ensuring it responds the right way in the right contexts.
It provides a complete view of the technical architecture behind agent customization, giving both business and technical audiences a clear blueprint for building their own intelligent automation assets.

Who This Series Is For

This content is designed to support solution consultants, implementation teams, architects, and business leaders who want to move beyond conceptual discussions and truly understand what agents can deliver in real processes.
If your organization is preparing for an adoption journey, a customer demo, or a deeper technical exploration, these videos provide a foundation you can build on right away.

Final Thoughts

AI‑driven agents are rapidly becoming core to SAP’s customer experience strategy. As their capabilities expand, so will the expectations from customers and internal teams alike. Understanding how to use, adapt, and build agents now will help organizations stay ahead of that curve.
I hope this series serves as a practical guide and inspires new possibilities for automation in your sales and service workflows.
Here is the playlist again for easy access:
https://learning.sap.com/video-playlists/exploring-and-configuring-joule-agents-in-sap-cx

entry.title: Re: Physical Stock Negative
entry.content:
First off :

  • Did the system generate a ShortDump, an ErrorMessage or anything as a starting point for your issue?
    If so, please provide the info ... (But assuming it did not ...)
  • Was a basic check via Prgr. "/SCWM/ERP_STOCKCHECK" performed?
    What was the outcome?

Next :
Have you checked for SAP-KBA's on your issue?
Please consider the following suggestions :
3079956 - CHM: Physical stock inconsistencies with negative quants - SAP for Me
3362742 - /scwm/r_lime_consistency_check - report to scan and correct LIME inconsistencies - SAP for Me
3629852 - Superfluous /SCWM/QUAN entry created in storage type that allows negative stock - SAP for Me
1915719 - Correction report for physical stocks - SAP for Me
3204028 - LIME consistency check report and must to have notes - SAP for Me
3588458 - There are inconsistencies in stock between IM and EWM - SAP for Me

From the above resources, you can derive a number of (analysis / correction) reports and suggestions. Check if Prgrs. such as "/SCWM/CORRECT_QUANTS" and "/SCWM/R_LIME_CONSISTENCY_CHECK" can be useful for ANALYSIS / DETECTION of the inconsistency. Please evaluate what these Reports do before executing them (you need to be sure the report only DISPLAYS data or also CORRECTS / UPDATES a table)!

If the above approaches did not provide any solution, please do as determined in SAP-KBA "3588458" :
"If the issue still cannot be resolved using the Compare Stock with Inventory Management app (= Prgr. "/SCWM/ERP_STOCKCHECK"), raise a ticket to SAP under the component SCM-EWM-IF-2CL."

Hope this helps

Nic T.

B. R. Amanda

entry.title: Re: Mass uploading Service Entry Sheets (SES) in SAP S/4H...
entry.content:
Please check information on App "F2027" and SAP-Help, but I do believe there is no Excel Template function.
As an alternative for S/4 Cloud Private, you could consider the use of "MM_PUR_SES_UPLOAD" App ...

Hope this helps

Nic T.
